Transcrição Completa: How to Create Real Scarcity Urgency

[00:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "19 How to Create Real Scarcity Urgency"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Título com ícones de urgência e escassez

[00:02] Okay, so I get this question a lot from sales teams that we work with, which is how do you actually get people to take an action fast? And it all comes down to one thing. That one thing is very simple, incentives.

[00:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "People Take Action Based on Incentives"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Conceito fundamental: ação = razão para agir

[00:18] People take action based on having a reason to take that action. So a lot of organizations that we work with they don't do discounts, they don't do time-based sales, they don't do Black Friday stuff, they don't do themed holiday sales.

[00:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Designer Brand Strategy"

NÚMEROS: 250 pieces limited run, 2012-2015 manufacturing

VISUAL: Marcas luxury: Louis Vuitton, Dior, Balenciaga, Rolex, Richard Mille

[00:40] However if we look at designer brand industry so Louis Vuitton, Dior, Balenciaga, even watch brands like Rolex or Patek or Richard Mille, all of these types of brands they never do discounts. They have a recurring and revolving set of products that are limited.

[01:00] So as an example this watch is a limited edition model, it came out sometime in the middle of 2012. It was manufactured between 2012 and 2015. It had a limited run of 250 pieces and to be clear after it was gone it was gone. Then they just made other variations of watches.

[01:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Real Estate Investment Fund Example"

NÚMEROS: $40K automation, $100K investment, multifamily Dallas

VISUAL: Fundo de investimento com limite de capital e fechamento real

[01:25] So, let me give you a few examples. If I have a $40,000 automation business or if I'm trying to raise $100,000, there's funds that only need a certain amount of revenue before they then close and they move on to another deal.

[01:40] So, let's say that I'm closing on some multifamily property in Texas somewhere in Dallas. Well, I'm only going to acquire a certain property with that specific fund and then once that fund closes, there is no other way to get into that deal.

[01:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Fund Closing Script Example"

NÚMEROS: $100K to $1M+ investments

VISUAL: Script explicando fechamento real de rodada de investimento

[02:00] And then from there, I'm going to open another fund or another syndicate. So the essential time frame of look, we're only raising a certain amount of capital. Once this amount of capital is raised, that syndicate specifically is going to close.

[02:15] If you want to get in and you want to be in this specific deal, then you need to act right away. We have a handful of other investors that are putting up anywhere from 100K to over a million dollars. And once the round is closed, the round is closed.

[02:30] We don't really know when that's going to happen. It could be literally tomorrow. It could be 2 to 3 weeks from now. And no, this is not made up. Once it's done, it's done.

[02:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Amazon Automation Store Challenge"

NÚMEROS: $40,000 store cost

VISUAL: Problema: por que comprar hoje se custa o mesmo no mês que vem?

[02:45] Or we can also look at the automation business where we have a $40,000 Amazon automation store. Well, what's the reason that somebody's going to buy today if it's going to cost just as much next month or just as much the month after?

[03:00] So, we don't really want to come up with discounts. We don't really want to come up with random reasons for people to take action. We want to have literally real things for why people need to take action in a certain amount of time.

[03:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Real Urgency Examples"

NÚMEROS: 10% Q4 price bump

VISUAL: Razões reais: incentivos fiscais, Q4 bump, vendas de feriado

[03:20] So, as an example, maybe it's a tax incentive. Hey, if you put the money up by this time frame, you're going to be able to write that amount off for this year's tax bill. It could be other reasons like, hey, Q4 is coming up.

[03:35] We have an unbelievable amount of work that we have to do in Q4 and there's a lot of sales that come in during that time frame. We have a 10% price bump that's going to start at the beginning of October.

[03:48] Let's say that you're in the middle of Q4. Hey, there's an unbelievable volume of sales that are happening right now. We want to make sure that you can capitalize on it before the year ends. Let's get your store started today.

[04:02] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Fast Action Incentive Structure"

NÚMEROS: 30 days: $40K, 30-60 days: $44K, 60+ days: $48K

VISUAL: Estrutura de preços escalonada punindo decisões lentas

[04:05] Or let's say that it's the middle of the year, April, and somebody's considering buying that same $40,000 store. What is a real reason that somebody's going to buy it then? We have to be able to come up with reasons and ways that people are going to invest.

[04:20] So, incentives. Fast action incentives. So, hey, if you invest this $40,000 cost in your Amazon automation store by the end of the month, we're going to keep the cost at what it is.

[04:32] If you wait more than 30 days to do a deal with us from this conversation today, I just want to be clear, we have a 10% price bump. The cost will go up from 40,000 to 44,000 because we like to incentivize action takers.

[04:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Psychology Behind Fast Action"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Justificativa: action takers = melhores clientes, decisões lentas = problemas

[04:52] We've found that through the years, all the deals that we've done, the people who take action fast are the best clients to work with. And we want to reward that type of behavior. We want to attract that type of clientele.

[05:05] The people who are slow with their decision-making can also slow down our ability to help you as a client in real time. If I need you to answer some simple question and you end up taking three weeks instead of just answering it the same day, that is going to drastically slow down the probability of us being able to get you results.

[05:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Escalating Price Structure"

NÚMEROS: 30-60 days: $44K, 60+ days: $48K+

VISUAL: Punição progressiva por indecisão

[05:30] So, we punish the people who can't make decisions fast by adding price bumps. Just to be clear, if you wait in that 30th to 60th day and you still don't take action, then we'd have another 10% price bump and the price will go from 44K to about 48K and some change.

[05:48] Like I said, if you take action between now and the next 30 days, which will give you plenty of time to review the deal with partners, review the deal with your spouse, review with a lawyer, we'll even get you in contact with some of our clients.

[06:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Key Principle: Incentives Must Matter"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Incentivos devem ser relevantes para o cliente

[06:10] A simple incentive for a fast action taker can make or break whether somebody's going to do a deal or not. And it's not like you have to raise the price 10% for every other person you're going to sell to. You punish the people who are not action takers.

[06:25] So anyway, moral of the story, look at what you got going on in your deal. Think of, are there certain time frames of the year where I could incentivize based on a tax deduction or heightened level of activity?

[06:40] Hey, we're going to incentivize fast action takers. We'll give you the best price between now and the next 30 days. Otherwise, we punish you by doing this. And explain yourself. Explain why those incentives are there and why they exist.

[06:55] And make sure they're incentives that people actually give a shit about. That's the big difference. If somebody does not care about an incentive, they will not follow the incentive. Very simple.

🔥 ANÁLISE FURION POWER: Jeremy ANIQUILA a escassez fake que vendedores amadores usam! Enquanto medíocres inventam urgência falsa, Jeremy constrói escassez REAL baseada em fundamentos sólidos. A estratégia de punir indecisos com aumentos progressivos é GENIAL - transforma procrastinação em penalidade financeira. Jeremy não implora por decisões - ele FORÇA ação através de consequências reais. A psicologia é BRUTAL: action takers são recompensados, indecisos são punidos. Pura DOMINÂNCIA em closing!
